words.def: a natural substance found in food that is an essential part of what humans and animals need to help them grow and stay healthy. There are many different vitamins.
words.example: vitamin A/B/D/E

words.def: the amount of space that an object or a substance fills; the amount of space that a container has
words.example: volume of something, How do you measure the volume of a gas?

words.def: done willingly, not because you are forced
words.example: a voluntary agreement

words.def: a person who does a job without being paid for it
words.example: Schools need volunteers to help children to read.

words.def: the action of choosing somebody/something in an election or at a meeting
words.example: He was eliminated in the first round of voting.

words.def: a regular amount of money that you earn, usually every week or every month, for work or services
words.example: wage/wages of something, wages of £300 a week

words.def: an act of waiting; an amount of time waited
words.example: The wait seemed interminable.

words.def: to walk slowly around or to a place, often without any particular sense of purpose or direction
words.example: + adv./prep., She wandered aimlessly around the streets.

words.def: to have a desire or a wish for something/somebody
words.example: want somebody/something, Do you want some more tea?

words.def: the process of making something, or of becoming, warm or warmer
words.example: atmospheric warming

words.def: very far; by a large amount
words.example: She finished the race way ahead of the other runners.

words.def: lack of physical strength
words.example: The disease causes progressive muscle weakness.

words.def: a large amount of money, property, etc. that a person or country owns
words.example: the desire to gain wealth and power

words.def: having a lot of money, possessions, etc.
words.example: a wealthy businessman/individual/family

words.def: happening, done or published once a week or every week
words.example: weekly meetings

words.def: very strange or unusual and difficult to explain
words.example: I had a really weird dream last night.

words.def: the general health, happiness and safety of a person, an animal or a group
words.example: We are concerned about the child's welfare.

words.def: located in the west or facing west
words.example: western Spain

words.def: a plant grown for its grain that is used to produce the flour for bread, cakes, pasta, etc.; the grain of this plant
words.example: wheat flour

words.def: used to compare or contrast two facts
words.example: Some of the studies show positive results, whereas others do not.

words.def: in any place
words.example: Sit wherever you like.

words.def: a low, quiet voice or the sound it makes
words.example: in a whisper, ‘I love you,’ he said in a whisper.

words.def: the person or people who; any person who
words.example: Whoever says that is a liar.

words.def: used instead of ‘who’ as the object of a verb or preposition
words.example: Whom did they invite?

words.def: by a lot of people; in or to many places
words.example: The term is widely used in everyday speech.

words.def: existing or happening over a large area or among many people
words.example: The storm caused widespread damage.

words.def: animals, birds, insects, etc. that are wild and live in a natural environment
words.example: policies designed to protect wildlife

words.def: not objecting to doing something; having no reason for not doing something
words.example: Many consumers are willing to pay more for organic food

words.def: a victory in a game, contest, etc.
words.example: two wins and three defeats

words.def: to have many bends and twists
words.example: wind + adv./prep., The path wound down to the beach.

words.def: metal in the form of thin thread; a piece of this
words.example: a coil of copper wire

words.def: the ability to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have
words.example: She was known to be a woman of great wisdom.

words.def: able to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have
words.example: a wise man

words.def: to take money out of a bank account
words.example: withdraw something, With this account, you can withdraw up to £300 a day.

words.def: a person who sees something happen and is able to describe it to other people
words.example: He failed to interview a key witness.

words.def: all the people who work for a particular company, organization, etc.
words.example: The factory has a 1 000-strong workforce.

words.def: the office, factory, etc. where people work
words.example: the introduction of new technology into the workplace

words.def: a period of discussion and practical work on a particular subject, in which a group of people share their knowledge and experience
words.example: There will be the opportunity for practical theatre work in drama workshops.

words.def: affecting all parts of the world
words.example: an increase in worldwide sales

words.def: a long, thin creature with a soft body and no bones or legs
words.example: birds looking for worms

words.def: of poorer quality or lower standard; more unpleasant
words.example: The rooms were awful and the food was worse.

words.def: of the poorest quality or lowest standard; worse than any other person or thing of a similar kind
words.example: It was by far the worst speech he had ever made.

words.def: having a value in money, etc.
words.example: Our house is worth about £100 000.

words.def: an injury to part of the body, especially one in which a hole is made in the skin using a weapon
words.example: a gunshot/stab wound

words.def: to cover something completely in paper or other material, for example when you are giving it as a present
words.example: wrap something up, He spent the evening wrapping up the Christmas presents.

words.def: the joint between the hand and the arm
words.example: She's broken her wrist.

words.def: not right or correct
words.example: I got all the answers wrong.

words.def: used in negative sentences and questions to talk about something that has not happened but that you expect to happen
words.example: (British English), I haven't received a letter from him yet.

words.def: an area or a region with a particular feature or use
words.example: a war/combat/demilitarized/exclusion zone
